sections.heading: Injection Molding
sections.text: Injection Molding High-speed plastic part production with tight tolerances and long-term tooling stability. Ideal for end-use parts that demand consistency and volume. Learn More

sections.heading: CNC Machining
sections.text: CNC Machining Precise, repeatable machining for metals and plastics. It is a great solution for production-grade geometries with demanding tolerances. Learn More

sections.heading: 3D Printing
sections.text: 3D Printing Agile and scalable for bridge production, custom runs, or complex geometries without upfront tooling costs. Learn More

sections.heading: Sheet Metal Fabrication
sections.text: Sheet Metal Fabrication Fast-turn parts like brackets and enclosures, fabricated with consistency for production volumes. Learn More

sections.heading: Production Materials
sections.text: Production Materials Protolabs supports a wide range of materials across metals, plastics, elastomers, and more. Our in-house inventory and global network help you source the right material fast, whether you're building functional prototypes or scaling to production.

sections.heading: Quality at Any Quantity
sections.text: Quality at Any Quantity You get reliable parts without traditional outsourcing risks. Protolabs maintains strict quality standards across our factories and network partners. CMM inspection report First Article Inspection (FAI) Dimensional Inspection Report (DIR) PPAP Material Certificates + Certificate of Analysis Conflict Minerals Reporting REACH + RoHS Certificates Prop65 Industry Certifications: ISO 9001 and AS 9100 Certificate of Conformance (CoC) Industry Certifications (Protolabs Network): ISO 13485, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, and ISO TS16949

sections.heading: Benefits of Outsourcing Manufacturing with Protolabs
sections.text: Benefits of Outsourcing Manufacturing with Protolabs Outsourcing manufacturing with Protolabs provides a streamlined and efficient way to produce high-quality parts at any scale. On-demand manufacturing provides access to fast, flexible production capabilities while maintaining quality, speeding up the transition from prototyping to full-scale production.

sections.heading: Seamless from Start to Scale
sections.text: Seamless from Start to Scale We help you bridge the gap between prototyping and production, with a consistent experience at every stage. One supplier for low- to high-volume manufacturing Keep production moving without switching vendors or repeating approvals Predictable quality, consistent communication

sections.heading: Built-In Agility
sections.text: Built-In Agility Adjust fast to market changes, volume swings, and design updates without losing momentum. Industry’s fastest lead times High-mix, low-volume readiness Global reach and localized support

sections.heading: Lower Risk, Higher Control
sections.text: Lower Risk, Higher Control From supply chain disruption to quality slips, traditional outsourcing can be risky. We mitigate that risk through clear standards, production visibility, and expert oversight. Certified systems and production-grade documentation Transparent pricing and digital workflows Trusted, audited manufacturing partners

sections.heading: How the Production Process Works
sections.text: How the Production Process Works We want to make partnering with us on a production project as smooth and streamlined as possible.

sections.heading: Step 1
sections.text: Step 1 Talk with a manufacturing expert who will help customize your production quote around your full bill of materials.

sections.heading: Step 2
sections.text: Step 2 Work with our team to optimize your project for production, including design for manufacturability and cost-down options.

sections.heading: Step 3
sections.text: Step 3 An initial sample run of parts is manufactured and sent to you to ensure parts meet your standards before the full production begins.

sections.heading: Step 4
sections.text: Step 4 Get process transparency and quality control as full production begins.

sections.heading: Step 5
sections.text: Step 5 Post-processing and final quality measures are applied before parts are shipped.

sections.heading: Step 6
sections.text: Step 6 Place subsequent batch production orders for the project at any time with our virtual warehouse.

sections.heading: Production Applications
sections.text: Production Applications Many industries decide to outsource their production manufacturing when flexibility, fast turnaround, or specialist capabilities are essential. Here's how Protolabs supports different sectors:

sections.heading: Medical
sections.text: Medical Tight regulations and rapid innovation make outsourcing ideal for small-batch devices, enclosures, or diagnostic parts. Protolabs' certified quality and documentation helps medical teams scale production reliably. Learn More

sections.heading: Aerospace
sections.text: Aerospace This industry often needs high-mix, low-volume production with extreme quality and traceability. Outsourcing with Protolabs ensures precision and compliance for spares, certification units, and new programs without tying up internal resources. Learn More

sections.heading: Automotive
sections.text: Automotive Car makers and suppliers outsource production of service parts, low-volume components for model variants, and bridge tooling between design and mass production. It helps reduce tooling investment and move faster when volumes don't justify in-house setups. Learn More

sections.heading: Industrial Equipment
sections.text: Industrial Equipment This sector benefits from outsourced production when developing new products or supporting custom installations. It enables short-run builds, specialty materials, and fast delivery without setting up dedicated lines. Learn More

sections.heading: Consumer Electronics
sections.text: Consumer Electronics Brands outsource production for different model variants or accessory parts that need to change frequently. Outsourcing avoids overproduction and allows fast pivots when specifications shift. Learn More
